fix(model-3d): fix split face extrusion and visual artifacts

Three fixes for push-pull after face splitting:
1. Push-pull preview now only moves the selected face's vertices (via
   triangleIndices) instead of all coplanar vertices on the plane
2. Removed computeVertexNormals() after kernel extrude — toBufferGeometry
   already provides correct flat normals; smooth normals caused visible
   triangulation seams
3. Face selection highlight and double-click now use HEMesh-aware face
   detection (buildFaceDataFromHE) instead of coplanar scan, so individual
   split faces are correctly identified

commit.message:
fix(model-3d): fix splitFace origins, extrude wall winding, and orphan cleanup

- Fix splitFace: swap split HE origins (endV→startV for face A, startV→endV for face B)
  to prevent duplicate vertices in face loops causing degenerate triangulation
- Fix extrude Case 2/3: remove direction-dependent wall winding ternary, always use
  [oldA, oldB, newB, newA] for consistent twin relationships
- Add detachBoundaryHE for cleaning up orphaned boundary HEs after extrude Case 3
- Unlink boundary HEs from chain before deletion in addFace to prevent stale pointers
- Add stricter validate(): twin origin consistency + duplicate vertex detection

All 64 kernel tests pass.

Fix email verification and password reset blocked by tenant middleware

Add verify-email and reset-password routes to PUBLIC_ROUTES so token-based
unauthenticated links work without a school context in the JWT.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent bad BTS coords from persisting in SQLite database

Root cause: import_from_billing_records() stored raw BTS values (DDMMSS
integers like 513507, sentinel -1) directly as decimal degrees in the
persistent bts.db SQLite database. Strategy 2 (BTS DB lookup) then
returned these bogus entries without validation, causing phantom map
points (e.g. -1,-1 in Gulf of Guinea) that survived server restarts.

Changes:
- bts_db.py: rewrite import_from_billing_records() to use shared DDMMSS
  parsing (_parse_bts_value) and coord order detection; reject sentinel
  LAC/CID ≤ 0 and UNKNOWN city; add cleanup_bad_coords() method
- geolocation.py: add _is_plausible_wgs84() validation to strategy 2
  BTS DB lookup results; skip records with LAC/CID ≤ 0
- gsm.py: pre-filter sentinel values before auto-import; call
  cleanup_bad_coords() on each parse to remove legacy bad entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Sends push notification + Resend email at J-7 and Jour J
- Notification toggle in profile tab with switch UI
- Push permission prompt shown after first donation registration
- Branded HTML email template with LifeDrop design

Env vars needed:
- NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY
- VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY
- SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
- CRON_SECRET
- RESEND_API_KEY (optional, emails skipped if not set)

  aneurysm_probability + 13-location classification; graceful fallback when
  weights unavailable

- Dockerfile.render: python:3.11-slim for Render CPU-only free tier

commit.message:
fix: apply ISO-guard to pico-fleet create_reminder to prevent bare-time UTC bypass (#129)

The fleet worker create_reminder case had the same raw bypass bug as
action-executor.ts: if taskConfig.datetime was a bare LLM-extracted time
string like "12:30" or "12.30", it was stored as-is (UTC), ignoring the
user's IANA timezone.

Applied the same ISO-guard pattern: only trust taskConfig.datetime directly
when it contains 'T' or starts with /^\d{4}-/; otherwise route through
parseReminderTime(rawDatetime, userTimezone) so Luxon applies the correct
timezone before UTC conversion.

Added source-inspection test to phase110.test.ts to lock the fix as a
regression guard.

Co-authored-by: trendywink247-afk <trendywink247-afk@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: move project paths from DB to config.json (#10)

Project collections now store their paths in config.json under a
"projects" map (mirroring code_groups), instead of in the database
collections.paths field. This unifies path management so all collection
types are config-driven.

Changes:
- Add Projects map[string][]string to Config struct
- Rewrite `index project` to work like `index group` (reads from config)
- Rewrite `collections paths` CLI to read/write config for all types
  (obsidian, calibre, code groups, projects)
- Update MCP handleRagIndex to use config for project paths
- Stop storing paths in DB via IndexProject()
- Add Projects tab to GUI settings
- Update GUI services to use config instead of DB for project indexing
- Update CLAUDE.md and README.md

so tokens get credited even when webhook can't reach the server. Also removes

- Rebuild icon buttons to 44×44 with hitSlop={4} (Apple minimum touch target)

- Guest hero: gradient card with decorative circles, Create Free Account + Sign In buttons
- Authenticated users see greeting row only; council banner is now a tappable Pressable
- All content sections use consistent rail/railHeader/railContent pattern (16px padding)
- Move all inline style objects into StyleSheet.create — no inline style objects in render
- Map button uses router.push('/map') directly; notifications guard redirects guests to login

commit.message:
fix(laravel-best-practices): fix all 29 issues — accuracy, format, and code correctness

- Fix 6 Laravel accuracy issues: HasMiddleware interface (controller-middleware),
  routes/console.php scheduling (eloquent-pruning), Event::listen() in
  AppServiceProvider (arch-event-driven), remove ->passes() on ValidationRule
  (validation-custom-rules), remove false "deprecated" label on sometimes()
  (validation-conditional-rules), use app() instead of constructor injection
  in FormRequest (validation-after-hooks)
- Fix format issues: ## Bad Example / ## Good Example headers across all files
  and AGENTS.md; remove all emoji (❌ ✅) from code comments throughout
- Fix code correctness: split multiple namespace declarations into separate
  PHP blocks (arch-action-classes, arch-dto-pattern, controller-api-resources);
  add missing use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Hash (arch-dto-pattern);
  add missing use Illuminate\Support\Str (controller-api-resources);
  add missing use AuthorizationException (validation-form-requests);
  move JSON snippets out of PHP fenced blocks (arch-feature-folders,
  controller-api-resources); move Blade syntax to blade blocks
  (eloquent-accessors-mutators, eloquent-eager-loading); fix duplicate
  company_name array key (validation-conditional-rules); fix passedValidation()
  misconception (controller-form-requests)
- Delete duplicate files: db-eager-loading.md, valid-form-requests.md
- Update metadata: rulesTotal=29, version 1.1.0, fix abstract language

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: DASS-42 and ASRS scoring bugs in clinical profile

DASS-42:
- Save subscale scores (depression/anxiety/stress) separately in history
- Status eval now uses worst subscale against proper DASS-42 cut-offs
  instead of total score with arbitrary thresholds
- Old data (no subscales saved) falls back to total-based eval

ASRS:
- Score display in profile shows "5/6 Part A" for old data, "17/18" for new
- Progress bar uses correct max (6 for old data, 18 for new)

commit.message:
Handle double-encoded breach_categories in JSONB queries

312 of 316 rows store breach_categories as a JSON string containing
an array (double-encoded) rather than a native JSONB array. Added CASE
to detect scalar strings and parse them before passing to
jsonb_array_elements_text(), fixing "cannot extract elements from a
scalar" errors on stats and hubs endpoints.
